intro:
There is no plant called "Stravadium rubrum". It's possible that you may be referring to a different plant, such as Stravadium sanguineum or possibly another species such as  Digitalis purpurea (Foxglove) or  Digitalis lanata (Glandular Foxglove) which have similar names and have been referred to as  'Digitalis rubra' in some references.

However, if you are referring to Digitalis purpurea, here is a paragraph about it:

Digitalis purpurea, also known as Foxglove, is a biennial herbaceous plant in the family Plantaginaceae. The health benefits of Digitalis purpurea include its use in the treatment of heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure, as well as its potential antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Digitalis purpurea include cardiac glycosides such as digoxin and digitoxin, as well as other compounds like bufadienolides and saponins. The most common herbal preparations made with Digitalis purpurea include dried leaf extracts, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to treat heart conditions, although it is essential to use these preparations under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to the risk of toxicity.
